sglang.title: Turn off autotune for scaled mm for fp8 dynamic quant in torchao
sglang.author: jerryzh168
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary:
Currently we do autotune in torch.compile (max-autotune-no-cudagraph), that will call triton kernel autotune for fp8 dynamic quant, but it increases compilation time significantly and not much benefit for llama models, so we turn this off for now
Test Plan:
python3 -m sglang.bench_latency -

sglang.title: ROCm: Fix MoE padding for none FP8 cases
sglang.author: HaiShaw
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
As mentioned.
Modifications
Separate padding size handling to FP8 and None FP8 cases.

sglang.title: [router] add base_gpu_id server args & merged radix tree python reference
sglang.author: ByronHsu
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

This PR prepares for #2114 by:


Adding base_gpu_id server argument to set the device ID offset when starting TP server. This enables starting multiple TP servers with different base GPU IDs when launching several servers and the router together using a single command. This also eliminat

sglang.title: Fix #2037 - Context length check does not take into out pad tokens for visual models
sglang.author: jakep-allenai
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Found a bug a few days ago when running Qwen2-VL through VLLM: #2037
I have tracked down the issue to the fact that the context-length limit is not respected once you account for padding tokens in visual language models.
Ex. you might have a request like this "user: what is in the followi

sglang.title: Rename sglang.bench_latency to sglang.bench_one_batch
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
As we have many benchmark scripts now, having cleaner naming for them is better.

bench_one_batch.py: Benchmark the latency of running a single static batch without a server.
bench_one_batch_server.py: Benchmark the latency of running a single batch with a server.
bench_offline_throughput.py: Benchm

sglang.title: Benchmark with Pytorch Profiler easily
sglang.author: bjmsong
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Users can use PyTorch Profiler during benchmark easily with --profile. In this way, the trace file will not be too large.
Usgae Example
export SGLANG_TORCH_PROFILER_DIR=/root/sglang/profile_log
python -m sglang.launch_server --model-path ${model_path}
python -m sglang.bench_serving --back

To this end, we need to allow passing a list of feature_sample_layers, which are indices in the visual encoder whose hidden states will be concatenated and use

FIX #9989

Note that dynamic_image_size=False is equivalent to max_dynamic_patch=1, means that image is processed to only one patch (thumbnail).

vllm.title: [V1] Fix Compilation config & Enable CUDA graph by default
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR fixes a performance bug on V1 introduced by #10437, which disabled custom ops even when torch.compile was not used.
Also, this PR enables the piecewise CUDA graphs by default since #10237 was merged.

vllm.title: [Minor] Revert change in offline inference example
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR moves the change in #10392 to a new file offline_inference_cli.py, and revokes the changes in offline_inference.py. The purpose is to keep the offline_inference.py example as simple as possible, so that users with no background on vLLM can immediately understand it.

vllm.title: [platforms] absorb worker cls difference into platforms folder
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
part of #9268
every platforms should specify the worker class inside their own code.
in addition, the default case is auto , and this allows users to specify custom classes for extensibility (which i'm working on as part of an RLHF support).

vllm.title: Remove token-adding chat embedding params
vllm.author: noamgat
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This follows the discussion in:
#10501 (comment)
Where @DarkLight1337 agreed that its better to remove the parameters altogether.
In embedding, the model does not generate additional tokens on top of the input text. Therefore, all parameters like "should a start-of-assistant-turn token be added" don
